paper_title: Sediment and Isotope Analysis in the Andaman Sea

methods.description: Collection and description of sediment samples from the Andaman Sea and Marthaban shelf for analysis of sediment layers and composition.

steps.description: Sediment samples spanning the last 54 ka of a deep-sea core (SK-168/GC-01) from the Andaman Sea and four Marthaban shelf sediments off Myanmar river mouths and Arakan coast were analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from specified locations

methods.description: Measurement of isotopic compositions of 87Sr/86Sr and 143Nd/144Nd in carbonate-free and Fe-Mn oxide free lithogenic fraction of bulk sediments.

steps.description: An aliquot of ~300 mg of dry bulk sediment samples were leached in 20 ml 0.44 M acetic acid (buffered to pH5 by sodium acetate) in acid cleaned 50 ml centrifuge tubes on a shaker for 3 hrs at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: leach sediment samples with buffered acetic acid

steps.description: The samples were then centrifuged and the acid containing dissolved carbonate was discarded. This step was repeated five times with fresh addition of buffered glacial acetic acid each time until no bubbles were discerned.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge and discard dissolved carbonate, repeat until no bubbles

steps.description: The solid residue left over was washed incrementally three times with Milli-Q water, before the Fe-Mn oxides were removed by strong reductive leaching with 10 ml of 1 M HH in 25% acetic acid on a hotplate set at 90 °C for three hours.
steps.searchable_summary: remove Fe-Mn oxides with reductive leaching

steps.description: Samples were centrifuged and the residue was then rinsed four times with Milli-Q water, dried in an oven at 100 °C for a day and reweighed.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse, dry, and weigh sediment residue

steps.description: Approximately 100 mg of the detrital material was totally dissolved in a 3:1 ratio of double distilled concentrated HF: HNO3 in steel jacketed PARR vessels at 180 °C for 4 days.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve detrital material in HF and HNO3

steps.description: Dissolved samples were nitrated twice to expel fluorides and re-dissolved in 6 M HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: nitrate and re-dissolve samples in HCl

steps.description: Sr and Nd isotope ratios were measured using a Nu Plasma MC-ICPMS in static multi-collection mode.
steps.searchable_summary: measure Sr and Nd isotope ratios with MC-ICPMS

methods.description: Evaluation of concentrations of major, trace, and REE in the lithogenic fraction of the sediments.

steps.description: About 2 g salt free, oven dried powdered samples were treated initially with 20 ml 1N HCl for 4 hrs, then with additional 15 ml and agitated occasionally for every 30 minutes.
Once the reactions ceased, the residues were washed thoroughly with MilliQ water four times, centrifuged and dried in an oven at 60 °C.
Major elements were measured on Wavelength-Dispersive X-ray Fluorescence (XRF-WD) at the National Institute of Oceanography (NIO), Goa.
For trace and REE analyses, 50 mg of powdered samples were weighed into Teflon beakers and dissolved using a suprapure acid mixture of HF, HNO3 and HClO4.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve samples for trace and REE analysis

steps.description: Elemental concentrations were analyzed by Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometer (ICP-MS) at NIO, Goa.
